extra.reasoning: The input variable `dizziness` does not appear in any rule (r1-r7) of the decision system. The rules only reference rash, vomiting, syncope, back_pain, palpitations, confusion, and pallor. Therefore the value of `dizziness` has zero effect on which rules match, which rule wins conflict resolution, or whether the default applies. For any fixed assignment of the other 7 inputs, the computed acuity is identical whether dizziness is false or true. Flipping dizziness to true leaves acuity exactly unchanged, which satisfies "acuity must be no lower than before" (equality qualifies as no lower). Since this holds for every point in the input space, the monotonicity property holds.
